title: Proof Boundary — Self-Healing Systems Need Governance
supported_claims: Current platforms provide bounded self-healing behaviors such as restart, replica replacement, endpoint removal, and some recovery operations.
Self-healing has documented limitations and does not repair every underlying application or storage failure.
Governed remediation requires boundaries, monitoring, escalation, rollback or fallback, recovery procedures, and accountable ownership.
unsupported_claims: Any product-specific self-healing capability not revalidated against current primary documentation.
Any quantified claim about incident duration, reliability, or operational-cost reduction.
Any assertion that autonomous remediation is appropriate for every failure class.
